---
title: "Difference-in-Differences on the Toy Panel"
subtitle: "A five-frame fixture deck"
author: "Workflow Test Bed"
date: "June 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
bibliography: toy-lecture.bib
---

## Why a known DGP?

- A reviewer that has never caught a planted bug is untested.
- The toy panel hardcodes its treatment effect, so every estimate downstream has a ground truth.
- Staggered-adoption pitfalls are deliberately absent here: common timing keeps two-way fixed effects clean [@goodmanbacon2021].
- Heterogeneity-robust estimators are exercised elsewhere [@callaway2021].

## The estimand

The fixture outcome follows

$$
y_{it} = \alpha_i + \gamma_t + \tau \, D_{it} + \varepsilon_{it},
\qquad \varepsilon_{it} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1),
$$

with $D_{it} = \mathbf{1}\{i \text{ treated}\} \cdot \mathbf{1}\{t \ge 2015\}$.

::: {.keybox}
**Key point.** The true effect is $\tau = 2$ by construction; the seeded
reference estimate is $\hat{\tau} = 1.71$ (cluster SE $0.37$).
:::

## Design timeline

Counties 11--20 are treated from 2015 onward; counties 1--10 are never
treated. (The Beamer source renders this as a TikZ timeline; the parity
test extracts it to SVG.)

## Summary

- The DGP equation is the single source of truth for every test.
- Balanced panel: 20 counties $\times$ 10 years $= 200$ observations.
- Regenerate with `make_fixtures.py` (seeded).

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
